ORDER OVERRULING DEMURRER

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT THE SALVATION ARMY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. A DEMURRER CAN ONLY BE USED TO CHALLENGE DEFECTS THAT APPEAR ON THE FACE OF THE PLEADING UNDER ATTACK OR FROM MATTERS OUTSIDE THE PLEADING THAT ARE JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE. (BLANK V. KIRWAN (1985) 39 CAL.3D 311, 318.) THE COURT THEREFORE CANNOT CONSIDER THE DECLARATION OF PATRICK G. ROSE OR ITS EXHIBITS IN RULING ON THE DEMURRER. COURT TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL O'REILLY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JULY 24, 2014 TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JULY 21, 2014 AT 4:00 P.M. OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. * * * MR. HOPKINS WHO APPEARED THOURGH COURT CALL PRIOR TO THE HEARING SAID HE WILL ARRANGE ANOTHER COURT CALL APPEARANCE ON JULY 24, 2014 * * * (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL O'REILLY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE PREMISES LIABILITY CAUSE OF ACTION. THERE IS NO ALLEGATION THAT DEFENDANT O'REILLY OWNED OR CONTROLLED THE PREMISES AND IT APPEARS THAT THAT CAUSE OF ACTION IS PROPERLY ALLEGED ONLY AGAINST THE SALVATION ARMY. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS THAT DEFENDANT PROVIDED FALSE TESTIMONY SUPPORTS THE CLAIM. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-22-2016 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Robert Porter, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff.

Having reviewed the pleadings and history of the case, the Court orders CASE DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE. The court finds no good cause or substantial justification for failure to comply with the previous court orders. A less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance.

Counsel to notify Department 302 re dismissal of the case in Department 610. Motion to dismiss after expiration of the time to amend following the sustaining of a demurrer to be heard on DEC-12-2016 in Department 302 shall be taken off calendar.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff KEVIN HOPKINS, Tel No. (510) 551-4101 present in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Plaintiff did not timely or properly notify the Court or the opposing party of his intent to appear. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT THE SALVATION ARMY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION, the motion is granted. The release/waiver of liability bars this action. In addition, plaintiff failed to provide an opposing separate statement of disputed facts and that is grounds alone to grant the motion. See CCP 437c(b)(3). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-24-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MICHAEL O'REILLY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the premises liability cause of action. There is no allegation that defendant O'Reilly owned or controlled the premises and it appears that that cause of action is properly alleged only against The Salvation Army. The demurrer is overruled as to the negligence cause of action. Plaintiff's allegations that defendant provided false testimony supports the claim. (Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-10-2014 Continued To Jul-24-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT MICHAEL O'REILLY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to July 24, 2014 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 21, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported.

* * * Mr. Hopkins who appeared thourgh court call prior to the hearing said he will arrange another court call appearance on July 24, 2014 * * *

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - KEVIN HOPKINS, TEL. NO. (707) 451-0782 EXT. 3263, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE SALVATION ARMY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. A demurrer can only be used to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) The Court therefore cannot consider the Declaration of Patrick G. Rose or its exhibits in ruling on the demurrer. Court to prepare order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
